Urgench branch of Tashkent Medical Academy, Uzbekistan

Atlas Mentor specializes in providing comprehensive guidance and support to students navigating their MBBS study abroad journey, with a particular focus on the Urgench Branch of Tashkent Medical Academy in Uzbekistan.

# Uzbekistan

Urgench Branch of Tashkent Medical Academy, Uzbekistan

The Urgench campus of the Tashkent Medical Academy is a key part of Uzbekistan’s medical education system. Known for its excellent medical programs, comprehensive curriculum, and dedicated faculty, it emphasizes both academic and practical learning, preparing students to excel in the medical field. Graduates from this branch have made significant contributions to healthcare in Uzbekistan and beyond. The campus is popular among aspiring medical students due to its commitment to quality, modern facilities, and research opportunities.

Established on July 19, 2005, by the president of Uzbekistan, the Urgench Branch initially offered programs in General Medicine, Pedagogy, and Nursing, with a capacity of 4,662 students. The academy also educates around 765 postgraduates and 205 physicians. The MBBS program focuses on producing highly trained professionals through clinical skills training. The institute conducts research under a grant on the environment and human health, coordinated by seven scientific councils.

The branch originated from the Khorezm Regional Center for advanced training of medical workers in 1992. In 1995, it was transferred to Urgench State University and became an independent educational institution in 1998.

The school features a 10-floor building with 90 rooms and 55 educational spaces, all equipped with e-book boards. There are 16 multimedia-equipped lecture rooms. Recognized by the World Health Organization, the school offers programs in Dentistry, Nursing, Medicine, and Pharmacy, and its international students can obtain certifications from various medical councils.

Why Choose Urgench Branch of Tashkent Medical Academy, Uzbekistan?

The Urgench branch of the Tashkent Medical Academy offers various amenities to meet the academic and extracurricular needs of students, including:

  • Modern Learning Spaces: Equipped with the latest technology and teaching tools.
  • Specialized Training Facilities: For various medical specialties.
  • Research Facilities: Available to support student interest in science and research projects.
  • Student Organizations: Numerous groups for extracurricular activities and personal growth.
  • Student Health Services: Medical aid and health services for students.
  • Sports and Recreation: Facilities for sports, relaxation, and leisure activities.

Key Features of Urgench Branch of Tashkent Medical Academy, Uzbekistan?

Course Duration: The complete MBBS course duration is 6 years, including a 12-month mandatory internship.

Affordable Education: World-class education offered at an affordable tuition fee.

Reasonable Hostel Charges: Affordable and reasonable accommodation costs.

International Cooperation: Collaboration with international scientific and educational institutions.

Clinical Exposure: Full exposure to medicine and clinical practice, with affiliated hospitals for training.

English Medium Instruction: Entire course taught in English.

Capital City Location: Located in the capital city of Uzbekistan, providing modern facilities.

Safety: The country is safe and secure for international students.

No Special Degree Required: NEET scorecard is sufficient for admission.

Ragging-Free Zone: A safe and supportive environment.

International Recognition: MBBS degree is internationally recognized.

Easy Transportation: Convenient access to transportation.

Financial Assistance: Scholarship programs available for students.

Hostel Accommodation: Hostel facilities provided for students.

High Security: The institute ensures a high level of security for students

Affiliation and Recognition of Urgench Branch of Tashkent Medical Academy, Uzbekistan

Tashkent Medical Academy has established partnerships with several foreign departments of education and research organizations. These collaborations aim to foster connections between leading higher education institutions in both the NIS (Newly Independent States) and other countries. This strategic approach enhances the academy’s educational quality and research capabilities.

Recognitions

  • World Health Organization (WHO): The academy is recognized globally for meeting international health education standards.
  • Medical Council of India (MCI): Ensures that the academy adheres to the medical education standards required for Indian students.
  • United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO): Affirms the academy’s commitment to educational excellence.

Approvals

  • National Medical Commission (NMC): The academy is approved by the NMC, making its degrees valid for practicing medicine in India.
  • WHO: Recognized for its adherence to global health education standards.

International Collaborations

  • Seoul National University: Partnership for academic and research collaborations in medical sciences.
  • Humboldt University (Berlin): Joint initiatives in medical education and research.
  • Russian State Medical University: Collaborative programs in medical training and research.
  • Samara State Medical University: Partnership to enhance clinical training and medical research.
  • Moscow State Medical University: Joint projects and student exchange programs.
  • Family Medicine Residency Center: Collaboration for residency training and practical experience in family medicine.
  • Chkhonnam State University (Korea): Exchange programs and joint research projects to enhance medical education.

These affiliations and recognitions ensure that Tashkent Medical Academy provides high-quality medical education that is recognized and respected worldwide. The academy’s partnerships with prestigious institutions enhance the learning experience, offering students opportunities for international exposure and collaborative research.

Eligibility Criteria of Urgench Branch of Tashkent Medical Academy, Uzbekistan

To secure admission to the Urgench Branch of Tashkent Medical Academy in Uzbekistan, applicants must meet specific eligibility criteria. These criteria ensure that students are adequately prepared for the rigorous medical education and training offered by the academy. Key requirements include age limitations, educational qualifications, and examination prerequisites, which collectively aim to maintain a high standard of academic excellence and competence among incoming students.

To gain admission to Tashkent Medical Academy, students must meet the following criteria:

  • Applicants must be at least 17 years old on or before December 31st of the admission year.
  • The upper age limit for applicants is 25 years.
  • Completion of 12th standard from a recognized Board of Education in India.
  • Required subjects: Physics, Chemistry, Biology, and English.
  • General category students must have a minimum of 50% in Physics, Chemistry, and Biology.
  • Reserved category students must have a minimum of 40% in Physics, Chemistry, and Biology.
  • Qualification in the NEET exam is mandatory.

Advantages of MBBS at Urgench Branch of Tashkent Medical Academy, Uzbekistan

Studying MBBS at Tashkent Medical Academy, Urgench Branch, Uzbekistan offers a range of significant advantages for aspiring medical professionals.

  • Accredited Programs: Recognized by WHO and approved by national medical authorities.
  • High-Quality Education: Emphasis on both theoretical and practical skills.
  • Experienced Faculty: Composed of healthcare experts and seasoned educators.
  • Modern Facilities: Contemporary classrooms, laboratories, and medical facilities.
  • Affordable Tuition Fees: More economical compared to many other countries.
  • English Language Programs: Accessible to international students without the need to learn a new language.
  • Cultural Exposure: Opportunity to meet people from diverse backgrounds and experience a new culture.
  • Welcoming Environment: Large international student body fostering a supportive atmosphere.
  • Clinical Experience: Access to real-world clinical training through various healthcare institutes.
  • Global Employment Opportunities: Graduates can find jobs in numerous countries.
  • Research Opportunities: Encourages participation in medical research.
  • Partnerships: Agreements with other medical schools or universities for expanded opportunities.
  • Student Safety: High priority on student safety and well-being; Uzbekistan is considered a safe destination.
  • Interactive Classes: Low teacher-to-student ratio for comprehensive learning.
  • Convenient Location: Located in Tashkent, the capital city, providing necessary facilities for studies and living.
  • Indian Mess and Food Facilities: Available for Indian students.
  • Direct Admission: No entrance exam or language proficiency tests like TOEFL or IELTS required.
  • Global Recognition: Degree recognized worldwide.
  • Advanced Infrastructure: Well-equipped classrooms and campus with top-class security and CCTV cameras.

Duration of MBBS at Urgench Branch of Tashkent Medical Academy, Uzbekistan

At the Tashkent Medical Academy, Urgench Branch in Uzbekistan, the MBBS (Bachelor of Medicine, Bachelor of Surgery) program typically spans six years. This comprehensive curriculum integrates five years of academic education with a mandatory final year dedicated to clinical training, preparing students for professional medical practice.

Documents Required Admission For MBBS at Urgench Branch of Tashkent Medical Academy, Uzbekistan

To apply for admission to Tashkent Medical Academy in Uzbekistan, the following documents are required:

  • SSC/10th Marks Memo: Certificate showing completion of secondary education.
  • HSC/12th Marks Memo: Certificate showing completion of higher secondary education with grades in relevant subjects.
  • Migration/Transfer Certificate (TC): Document indicating transfer from the previous educational institution.
  • Passport with at least 1-year validity: Valid passport ensuring legal stay throughout the course duration.
  • Passport-size color photographs: Recent photographs meeting the specified size and format requirements for official use.

Hostel And Accommodation at Urgench Branch of Tashkent Medical Academy, Uzbekistan

Tashkent Medical Academy offers well-equipped hostel facilities for its international students. Each hostel block includes common amenities such as kitchens and laundry rooms, ensuring convenience for daily living.

  • Facilities: Hostels are equipped with reading rooms, internet facilities, a library, and a gymnasium.
  • Security: Strict security measures are in place, with wardens ensuring safety and supervision.
  • Separate Accommodation: Boys’ and girls’ hostels are segregated.
  • Maintenance: Rooms and washrooms are regularly cleaned and furnished with necessary furniture and basic amenities.
  • Shared Basis: Accommodation is available on a shared basis, promoting a communal living environment.

These facilities and arrangements aim to provide a comfortable and conducive living environment for students pursuing their studies at Tashkent Medical Academy.

Career Opportunities for MBBS Graduates at Urgench Branch of Tashkent Medical Academy, Uzbekistan

Graduates of Tashkent Medical Academy’s MBBS program have diverse career opportunities both locally and internationally:

  • Medical Practice: Many graduates return to their native countries, such as India, to establish private practices, work in hospitals, or provide primary medical care.
  • Postgraduate Specialization: Graduates can pursue postgraduate programs (MD/MS) in fields like dermatology, internal medicine, pediatrics, radiology, surgery, and more, to specialize clinically.
  • Medical Education and Research: Some graduates opt for careers in medical education and research, contributing to advancements in healthcare knowledge and practice.
  • Hospital and Healthcare Administration: MBBS holders qualify to manage hospitals, clinics, and medical facilities, overseeing strategic planning and operational management.
  • Public Health: Those interested in public health can work in health policy, epidemiology, and disease control, contributing to community health improvement and disease prevention.
  • Healthcare Consulting: Graduates may work as healthcare consultants, advising hospitals and healthcare facilities on operational improvements, patient care, and service enhancements.
  • Medical Research: Opportunities exist in clinical trials and medical research, often in collaboration with pharmaceutical companies, academic institutions, or governmental health organizations.
  • Entrepreneurship: Some graduates choose to start their own clinics, diagnostic centers, or telemedicine ventures, leveraging their medical expertise and entrepreneurial spirit.
  • Global Opportunities: MBBS graduates can explore job opportunities in various countries that recognize their medical degrees earned in Uzbekistan, offering a global career path.

These diverse career paths showcase the versatility and breadth of opportunities available to MBBS graduates from Tashkent Medical Academy, preparing them for impactful roles in healthcare worldwide.
